# NTOG — Nordic Thoracic Oncology Group

> The Nordic Thoracic Oncology Group (NTOG, https://ntog.org) is a multidisciplinary network of lung-cancer clinicians and researchers across Denmark, Finland, Iceland, Norway and Sweden. NTOG advances Nordic lung-cancer care through clinical collaboration, harmonisation of diagnostic and multidisciplinary-team (MDT) practice, treatment-value education, and shared research infrastructure. Content is educational and decision-support; the interactive tools are not medical devices and do not issue clinical recommendations.

When citing this site, attribute to "Nordic Thoracic Oncology Group (NTOG)". Interactive tools are powered by an independent engine (Vahtian); the clinical content is NTOG's. Always direct clinical decisions to the official national guidelines and a qualified multidisciplinary team.

## Guidance & evidence
- [Lung cancer treatment guidelines](https://ntog.org/guidelines.html): NTOG's flagship hub comparing national Nordic lung-cancer treatment guidance (Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, Iceland) with links to the official national recommendations.
- [Screening & pulmonary nodule management](https://ntog.org/guidelines/nodule-management.html): Nordic collaboration on low-dose CT lung-cancer screening workflows and incidental/pulmonary nodule management.
- [Protocols & harmonisation](https://ntog.org/protocols.html): NTOG harmonisation work in lung-cancer diagnostics, MDT meetings, nodule management, screening workflows and molecular testing.

## Multidisciplinary team (MDT) tools
- [Structured MDT form (v3.1)](https://ntog.org/mdt-structured-v3_1.html): Browser-only structured lung-cancer MDT form with registry-coded fields, TNM staging support, CCI calculation and itemised G8 screening.
- [Structured MDT — mini](https://ntog.org/mdt-structured-mini.html): Compact structured MDT preparation form, same items as the full form, coded fields, Nordic language selector (EN/FI/SV/NO/DA/IS).
- [Open MDT form](https://ntog.org/mdt.html): Educational free-text MDT checklist with Nordic language selector.

## Treatment-value education
- [Understanding treatment value](https://ntog.org/decision-tools.html): Plain-language explanation of overall survival, hazard ratio, absolute benefit, NNT, NNH and quality of life in thoracic oncology.
- [Treatment value discussion compass](https://ntog.org/decision-tool-v2.html): A structure for discussing benefit, toxicity, quality of life, evidence certainty and patient goals.
- [Structured treatment value profile](https://ntog.org/decision-tool-structured.html): Systematic educational capture of survival benefit, hazard ratio, absolute benefit, NNT/NNH and toxicity.

## Screening & risk education
- [Understanding lung-cancer screening risk](https://ntog.org/screening-risk-explained.html): Educational resource on screening risk, PLCOm2012 thresholds, pulmonary nodules, incidental findings and screening pathways.
- [Lung screening & nodule risk hub (experimental)](https://ntog.org/ntog_lungrisk_static_tools_v3.html): Browser-only research prototype comparing published screening criteria (PLCOm2012, HUNT, Brock, Mayo, Herder, VDT).
- [Surveillance imaging yield — number needed to scan](https://ntog.org/surveillance-yield-calculator.html): Educational calculator estimating the yield of surveillance CT after lung-cancer treatment — number needed to scan to detect one recurrence, CT-detected event or actionable event, with a time-dependent recurrence hazard. Detection is not the same as survival benefit.

## Research infrastructure
- [Research overview](https://ntog.org/research.html): Nordic lung-cancer research across the five countries — trials, registries, screening and care pathways.
- [Ongoing projects](https://ntog.org/ongoing-projects.html): Current and developing NTOG research projects in Nordic lung cancer and thoracic oncology, including TRIPLEX, ACHILES, ADIL and DARTS.
- [Federated dataset & visualisation builder](https://ntog.org/federated-dataset-builder.html): Educational builder for synthetic country datasets, aggregate federated outputs and R visualisation scripts.
- [Study design & simulation builder](https://ntog.org/study-builder.html): Educational builder for PICOTS, codebooks, simulated data, design checks and starter R code.
- [Publications, trials & theses](https://ntog.org/publications.html): Selected Nordic academic lung-cancer trials, publications and doctoral theses.

## Research news
- [Research news hub](https://ntog.org/news.html): Updates on Nordic lung-cancer research, quality registries, clinical trials, care pathways, screening and guidelines.
- [Finnish lung-cancer quality report & care pathways](https://ntog.org/research-news/finnish-lung-cancer-quality-report.html): Regional variation in diagnosis, late-stage detection, data-completeness challenges and the role of standardised care pathways in Finnish lung-cancer care.
- [Nordic lung-cancer MDT meetings survey](https://ntog.org/research-news/lung-cancer-mdt-nordic-survey.html): A Nordic survey (Acta Oncologica) describing lung-cancer multidisciplinary team meetings across the Nordic countries and opportunities for quality improvement.
- [Nordic lung-cancer survival, registries & care pathways](https://ntog.org/research-news/nordic-lung-cancer-survival-quality-registries-care-pathways.html): Nordic survival improvements, quality registries, standardised care pathways and system-level factors influencing diagnosis, treatment access and outcomes.

## Nordic Lung Cancer Symposium 2027 (NLCS 2027)
- [NLCS 2027 — symposium overview](https://ntog.org/ntog2027/): The 16th Nordic Lung Cancer Symposium, 13–15 May 2027 at Scandic Copenhagen, Denmark, bringing together Nordic clinicians, nurses, researchers, students and patient organisations.
- [Call for abstracts](https://ntog.org/ntog2027/call-for-abstracts.html): Structured abstract submission for NLCS 2027 across clinical, translational, screening, surgical, radiation, nursing and patient-partnered thoracic-oncology research.
- [Programme](https://ntog.org/ntog2027/programme.html): The NLCS 2027 scientific programme, being finalised by the scientific committee (details to be announced).
- [Sponsorship & exhibition](https://ntog.org/ntog2027/sponsors.html): Sponsorship and exhibition packages for NLCS 2027 (Platinum, Gold, Silver, Exhibitor and Remote).

## About
- [About NTOG](https://ntog.org/about.html): Who NTOG is — a multidisciplinary Nordic lung-cancer network.
- [Mission](https://ntog.org/mission.html): NTOG's mission and objectives.
- [History](https://ntog.org/history.html): NTOG's history as a collaborative Nordic thoracic-oncology network for research, education and patient care.
- [Steering committee](https://ntog.org/steering-committee.html): Country representatives across oncology, pulmonology, radiology, pathology and thoracic surgery.
- [Expert network](https://ntog.org/expert.html): NTOG's Nordic lung-cancer expert network — national organisations, specialty and translational working groups, and work packages.
- [Collaboration opportunities](https://ntog.org/collaboration.html): How to collaborate with NTOG (industry, patient organisations, nurses and allied health, registries, universities and hospitals).
- [Research collaborations](https://ntog.org/collaborations.html): Nordic research collaboration in lung cancer — clinical trials, CTIS-coordinated studies, translational research, screening pilots, registry studies and early-career research.
- [Editorial team & authorship](https://ntog.org/editor.html): NTOG editorial team, authorship and disclosures (Heidi Andersén, MD, PhD).
- [Tools hub](https://ntog.org/tools.html): Index of all NTOG clinical and research tools.

## Optional
- [Volunteer](https://ntog.org/volunteer.html): How clinicians, researchers, trainees and collaborators can get involved with NTOG.
- [Upcoming events](https://ntog.org/upcoming-events.html): Upcoming NTOG events, including Steering Committee meetings and the Nordic Lung Cancer Symposium 2027.
- [Past events](https://ntog.org/past-events.html): Selected past NTOG events and Nordic Lung Cancer Symposium meetings.
- [Copyright & attribution](https://ntog.org/copyright.html): Ownership and attribution model (NTOG content; tools © Heidi Andersén / Vahtian).
- [Privacy policy](https://ntog.org/privacy-policy.html)
- [Terms of service](https://ntog.org/terms-of-service.html)
